questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT one of the Upper Basin states of the Colorado River Basin?
questions.quip_correct: AZ is Lower Basin — along with CA and NV.
questions.quip_wrong: Upper Basin: CO, NM, UT, WY. Arizona is Lower Basin.

questions.prompt: What geographic point divides the Colorado River Basin into the Upper and Lower Basins?
questions.quip_correct: Lees Ferry, Arizona — the legal and hydrologic dividing line.
questions.quip_wrong: It's Lees Ferry, AZ — set by the 1922 Colorado River Compact.

questions.prompt: Approximately what fraction of the Colorado River Basin's water supply originates in the Upper Basin?
questions.quip_correct: ~92% — the mountains of CO, WY, UT, and NM do the heavy lifting.
questions.quip_wrong: About 92% — the Upper Basin snowpack is the basin's water tower.

questions.prompt: The 2007 Interim Guidelines established shortage operations for Lake Mead. In what year were they set to expire?
questions.quip_correct: 2026 — triggering the Post-2026 Operations renegotiation process.
questions.quip_wrong: They expire in 2026, which is why the renegotiation process began in 2023.

questions.prompt: Approximately what percentage of America's winter leafy greens come from the Yuma, Arizona area?
questions.quip_correct: 90% — Colorado River water grows your winter salad.
questions.quip_wrong: About 90% of winter leafy greens come from Yuma, irrigated by the Colorado.

questions.prompt: What federal agency operates the major dams of the Colorado River Basin?
questions.quip_correct: Reclamation â€” Hoover, Glen Canyon, and many more.
questions.quip_wrong: The Bureau of Reclamation builds and operates the big dams.

questions.prompt: In the simulation-based optimization framework for Colorado River Basin management, which simulation software is used to model reservoir operations?
questions.quip_correct: RiverWare — developed right at CU Boulder's CADSWES.
questions.quip_wrong: RiverWare (riverware.org) is the river/reservoir simulation engine used.

questions.prompt: What multi-objective evolutionary algorithm (MOEA) is coupled with RiverWare to search for optimal operating policies in this Colorado River research?
questions.quip_correct: Borg MOEA — an adaptive, many-objective optimizer.
questions.quip_wrong: Borg MOEA is the optimizer — it drives the search for Pareto-optimal policies.

questions.prompt: What does DMDU stand for in the context of Colorado River Basin water management?
choices: Distributed Model of Demand and Use
questions.quip_correct: Deep Uncertainty — when you don't even know the probability distribution of futures.
questions.quip_wrong: DMDU = Decision Making Under Deep Uncertainty — stress-testing policies across many futures.

questions.prompt: In the Post-MORDM framework (Bonham et al. 2022), what unsupervised machine learning method organizes policies into a 2D map where nearby policies perform similarly?
questions.quip_wrong: The Self-Organizing Map (SOM) arranges policies so similar ones cluster together.

questions.prompt: In the MOEA policy representation for Lake Mead shortage operations, what is the maximum number of discrete shortage tiers that can be defined by the shortage elevation variables?
questions.quip_correct: 6 tiers — variables e1 through e6 each set a shortage elevation threshold.
questions.quip_wrong: Up to 6 shortage tiers (e1–e6), each with a corresponding volume reduction (V1–V6).
